text: Good design is as little design as possible. Less, but better.
author: Dieter Rams

text: Simplicity is not the absence of clutter; it's the presence of order.
author: Jonathan Ive

text: The user interface is the product. Everything behind it is infrastructure nobody asked for.
author: based

text: If you need a user manual for your UI, you've already failed.
author: based

text: Design is not just what it looks like and feels like. Design is how it works. And if it doesn't work, no amount of design saves it.
author: based

text: A pixel-perfect mockup of a broken flow is still a broken flow.
author: based

text: The best interface is no interface. The second best is one so obvious you forget it exists.
author: based

text: Nobody has ever complained that something was too easy to use.
author: based

text: Every pixel of whitespace is a deliberate choice to not add something stupid.
author: based

text: Your landing page has 7 CTAs. That's not a landing page, that's a panic attack.
author: based

text: If your color palette is more than 4 colors, you're decorating, not designing.
author: based

text: The best design system is the one your team actually uses, not the one that wins awards on Dribbble.
author: based

text: Every designer wants to make the world more beautiful. Then they get hired to darken a CTA button 2% darker for the 47th time.
author: based

text: The fastest way to find out if your design is good is to show it to someone who doesn't care about you.
author: based

text: A/B testing is how you find out which of your two bad ideas is slightly less bad.
author: based

text: If your design needs a tooltip to explain itself, redesign it.
author: based

text: Responsive design is easy. Responsive design that doesn't look like garbage on a 320px screen is hard.
author: based

text: The gap between what a designer hands off and what a developer builds is where user experience goes to die.
author: based

text: Dark mode isn't a feature. It's a survival mechanism.
author: based

text: The most underrated design tool is a napkin. The most overrated is Figma.
author: based

text: If your SaaS has a gradient hero section, a stock photo of a diverse team laughing at a laptop, and three columns of features nobody reads — congratulations, you've built the internet.
author: based

text: Every 'add more whitespace' suggestion is just 'I don't know how to fix this content' with better PR.
author: based

text: The best mobile app is one thumb, one eyeball, one task.
author: based

text: Your form has 15 required fields. You're not collecting data, you're conducting an interrogation.
author: based

text: Accessibility isn't a nice-to-have. It's the law and basic human decency. Pick whichever motivates you.
author: based

text: The best loading indicator is no loading indicator. The second best is one that's honest about how long it'll take.
author: based

text: Flat design died so that neumorphism could be born and also die.
author: based

text: Every design trend is just the previous trend with different border-radius.
author: based

text: Your hero section doesn't need a video background. Nobody is on your landing page for the cinematography.
author: based

text: The best UI component library is the one that gets out of your way. The worst is the one you have to fight.
author: based

text: There are two hard things in computer science: cache invalidation, naming things, and off-by-one errors.
author: Leon Bambrick

text: It works on my machine. Ship my machine.
author: based

text: I don't have a backup plan. I have 'recreate it worse from memory' plan.
author: based

text: A monorepo is just a repo that gave up on boundaries. A polyrepo is just a monorepo that gave up on simplicity.
author: based

text: The cloud is just someone else's computer that charges you rent and occasionally catches fire.
author: based

text: The best code review comment is 'LGTM' after 0 seconds of review. The most honest is 'I'll look at this tomorrow' repeated 47 times.
author: based

text: A SQL query walks into a bar, sees two tables, and asks: 'Can I JOIN you?'
author: based

text: There are 10 types of people in the world: those who understand binary and those who don't. And those who expected base-3.
author: based

text: The first 90% of the code accounts for the first 90% of the development time. The remaining 10% accounts for the other 90%.
author: Tom Cargill

text: If debugging is the process of removing bugs, then programming must be the process of putting them in.
author: Edsger W. Dijkstra

text: Programming is 10% writing code and 90% understanding why it doesn't work.
author: based

text: The code works. Don't touch it. I don't know why it works. Don't ask.
author: based

text: Premature optimization is the root of all evil. But premature abstraction is the root of all suffering.
author: based

text: Every Junior Dev: 'I'll just use a regex for this.' Every Senior Dev: 'I'll just use a regex for this.' Every Staff Dev: 'I'll just use a regex for this.' None of them have learned.
author: based

text: The best error message is the one that never appears. The second best tells me what I did wrong, not what the code did wrong.
author: based

text: Every time you write a comment explaining 'why', a senior dev somewhere nods. Every time you write a comment explaining 'what', a senior dev somewhere drinks.
author: based

text: If your code needs comments to be understood, rewrite the code. If your code needs comments to be maintained, document the architecture.
author: based

text: The best time to fix a bug is during code review. The second best time is never because you shipped it to production and nobody noticed.
author: based

text: Clean code always looks like it was written by someone who cares. Messy code looks like it was written by someone who's about to quit.
author: based

text: The only valid measurement of code quality is how many times you have to read it to understand it.
author: based

text: Technical debt is what you call it when you want to feel sophisticated about procrastinating.
author: based

text: A 10x developer isn't someone who codes 10x faster. It's someone who creates 10x less work for everyone else.
author: based

text: The worst part about being a senior developer isn't the complexity. It's knowing that the simple solution you proposed was right all along.
author: based

text: Rewriting from scratch is always tempting. It's also always a terrible idea. But you won't believe me until you've done it yourself.
author: based

text: The problem with Object-Oriented Programming is that it was invented by academics, and everything they do is overcomplicated.
author: based

text: Every framework is somebody's way of saying 'I disagree with how you write code' and then charging you for the privilege.
author: based

text: The best database schema is the one that answers your questions without making you write a 47-line SQL query.
author: based

text: Unit tests don't prevent bugs. They prevent the specific bugs you anticipated. Production finds the rest.
author: based

text: If you're writing boilerplate, you're not programming. You're typing. Find a better abstraction or a better language.
author: based

text: The best time to choose your database was yesterday. The second best time is before you have 10,000 users, not after.
author: based

text: TypeScript is just JavaScript wearing a trench coat and fake mustache pretending to be a serious language.
author: based

text: The best API is one where you don't need the docs. The second best is docs that are actually complete. Neither exists.
author: based

text: Every good abstraction starts as 'this is so simple!' and ends as 'I can't believe how complex this is.'
author: based

text: Your microservices aren't microservices. They're just modules that hate each other and communicate via HTTP.
author: based

text: The best time to set up CI/CD was when you started the project. The second best time is now. The worst time is after your third 'it works on my machine' incident.
author: based

text: If your README has more badges than content, your project is a resume builder, not software.
author: based

text: Every dev who says 'I don't need version control' has a story that starts with 'So I accidentally deleted production and...'
author: based

text: Code that's easy to delete is better than code that's easy to write.
author: based

text: The best code is no code at all. Every line of code is a liability.
author: Jeff Atwood

text: Simplicity is a prerequisite for reliability.
author: Edsger W. Dijkstra

text: The function of good software is to make the complex appear to be simple.
author: Grady Booch

text: First, solve the problem. Then, write the code.
author: John Johnson

text: Any fool can write code that a computer can understand. Good programmers write code that humans can understand.
author: Martin Fowler

text: The most dangerous phrase in the language is: 'We've always done it this way.'
author: Grace Hopper

text: Ship, then test. Not test, then ship. The market doesn't wait for your test suite.
author: based

text: The best time to refactor is when the code is fresh in your mind. The worst time is when you're debugging a production incident at 3 AM.
author: based

text: Every codebase is one junior dev away from a complete rewrite.
author: based

text: You can't refactor what you can't read. You can't read what you can't understand. Start there.
author: based

text: The difference between a senior and a junior dev isn't knowledge. It's knowing which shortcuts will haunt you later.
author: based

text: If your test suite takes more than 5 minutes, people won't run it. If people don't run it, it doesn't exist.
author: based

text: The best engineers aren't the ones who write the most code. They're the ones who delete the most code.
author: based

text: Legacy code is just code that nobody has the courage to delete yet.
author: based

text: Naming things is easy. Naming things well is a skill. Naming things in a way that still makes sense 2 years later is a superpower.
author: based

text: The best architects are the ones who can draw boundaries, not the ones who can draw boxes.
author: based

text: Stop building for scale you don't have. You're not Google. You have 47 users and 3 of them are your mom.
author: based

text: The best technology choice is the one your team knows. The second best is the one that's actually right. Usually they're different.
author: based

text: Your system design document should fit on one page. If it doesn't, your system design is too complicated.
author: based

text: The most expensive part of software isn't the code. It's the maintenance. Plan for it.
author: based

text: If you can't explain it to a six-year-old, you don't understand it. If your code can't be read by a junior dev, you haven't simplified it.
author: based

text: Every database migration is a small act of violence against your future self. Write the rollback first.
author: based

text: The worst kind of technical debt is the kind you don't know you have.
author: based

text: Deploy on Friday if you want. Just don't deploy on Friday afternoon and then leave.
author: based

text: The best alerting system is one that pages you about things that actually matter. The worst is one that pages you about everything so you ignore it all.
author: based

text: Your staging environment should be a copy of production. If it's not, it's a lie you tell yourself.
author: based

text: Kubernetes is what happens when sysadmins get bored and decide to turn 'turn it off and on again' into a distributed system.
author: based

text: Docker: because 'it works on my machine' stopped being acceptable. Now it works on everyone's machine but nobody understands why.
author: based

text: If you need a PhD to understand your deployment pipeline, you've built a research project, not a product.
author: based

text: Monitoring tells you what's broken. Observability tells you why. Most teams have neither.
author: based

text: The best incident response is the one where you don't need to respond because the system healed itself. The second best is one with a runbook.
author: based

text: Post-mortems are like therapy: everyone feels better afterward, but nothing actually changes.
author: based

text: Productivity is never an accident. It is always the result of commitment to excellence, intelligent planning, and focused effort.
author: Paul J. Meyer

text: The key is not to prioritize what's on your schedule, but to schedule your priorities.
author: Stephen Covey

text: Productivity is doing more of what matters. Not doing more things.
author: based

text: Your to-do list isn't a productivity tool. It's a guilt collection. Delete it and do the one thing that matters.
author: based

text: The most productive people aren't the ones who do the most. They're the ones who say no the most.
author: based

text: Meetings are where minutes are kept and hours are lost.
author: based

text: If your calendar is full, you're not productive. You're just busy. There's a difference.
author: based

text: The best productivity hack is a deadline. The second best is consequences.
author: based

text: You don't need a second monitor. You need fewer tabs.
author: based

text: The Pomodoro Technique is great until you enter flow state and your timer interrupts you to take a break you didn't need.
author: based

text: Inbox Zero is a lie. The goal isn't zero emails. It's zero emails that matter.
author: based

text: Every time management introduces a new 'productivity tool,' productivity drops by 30% while everyone learns it, then returns to baseline.
author: based

text: Batch your shallow work. Protect your deep work. Ignore everything else.
author: based

text: The best way to get something done is to begin. Not to plan. Not to research. Not to optimize. Begin.
author: based

text: Multitasking is a myth. You're just switching between tasks poorly and calling it efficiency.
author: based

text: The best note-taking system is the one you actually use. Not the one you spend 47 hours setting up.
author: based

text: Burnout isn't caused by working too hard. It's caused by working too hard on things that don't matter.
author: based

text: The most productive thing you can do today is delete half your to-do list.
author: based

text: Your morning routine doesn't matter if your evening routine is doomscrolling until 2 AM.
author: based

text: Hustle culture is just exploitation with better marketing.
author: based

text: The 5 AM club only works if you go to bed at 9 PM. Most people who join it just lose 2 hours of sleep and call it discipline.
author: based

text: Done is better than perfect. But also: good is better than done-but-broken.
author: based

text: If it takes less than 2 minutes, do it now. If it takes less than 2 hours, do it today. If it takes longer, schedule it and then don't schedule it.
author: based

text: Time management is really just priority management. If everything is a priority, nothing is.
author: based

text: The real productivity hack is caring less about productivity hacks and more about the work.
author: based

text: The best investment you can make is in yourself. The second best is in tools that save you time. The worst is in courses that save you nothing.
author: based

text: Revenue is vanity, profit is sanity, cash flow is reality.
author: based

text: A startup is a human institution designed to create something new under conditions of extreme uncertainty.
author: Eric Ries

text: The best time to start a business was yesterday. The second best time is now. The worst time is 'when the market is ready.'
author: based

text: Nobody cares about your startup. They care about their problem. If your startup solves it, they'll care about you.
author: based

text: Your first product should embarrass you. If it doesn't ship fast enough, it's too polished.
author: based

text: If you're not embarrassed by your first release, you launched too late.
author: Reid Hoffman

text: The market doesn't care about your tech stack. The market cares about whether you solve their problem.
author: based

text: Every business plan is fiction until the first customer pays. Then it's a hypothesis.
author: based

text: Don't find customers for your product. Find products for your customers.
author: Peter Drucker

text: The best marketing doesn't feel like marketing. It feels like someone telling you about something that will genuinely help you.
author: based

text: Bootstrapping isn't about being cheap. It's about being forced to find product-market fit before you run out of runway.
author: based

text: Raising money is easy. Building something people want is hard. Most founders get these confused.
author: based

text: Your competitors aren't your problem. Your customers' indifference is your problem.
author: based

text: Every successful business is a bet that the world is different than most people think.
author: based

text: The lean startup method is just the scientific method for people who failed biology.
author: based

text: First principles thinking sounds profound until you realize it's just 'ask why' without the philosophy degree.
author: based

text: Disruption isn't something you plan. It's something that happens to incumbents who were too comfortable.
author: based

text: The best exit strategy is the one you don't need because the business is printing money.
author: based

text: Vision without execution is hallucination.
author: Thomas Edison

text: The biggest risk is not taking any risk. In a world that is changing really quickly, the only strategy that is guaranteed to fail is not taking risks.
author: Mark Zuckerberg

text: Fail fast, fail forward, fail cheaply. But most importantly: learn.
author: based

text: Your network is your net worth. But your net worth is also your network's network's net worth. It's turtles all the way down.
author: based

text: The graveyard of failed startups is filled with products nobody asked for, built by people who didn't listen.
author: based

text: Pricing is how you tell the market what you're worth. Charge too little and you'll go out of business. Charge too much and you'll go out of business faster.
author: based

text: The best business model is one where the customer pays you before you have to do the work. Find it.
author: based

text: If your business can't survive without you, you don't have a business. You have a job.
author: based

text: Scarcity creates value. If everyone can do what you do, you're competing on price. If nobody can, you're competing on nothing.
author: based

text: The best businesses solve expensive problems for people who can afford to pay. Everything else is a hobby.
author: based

text: You don't need permission to start. You don't need a co-founder to start. You don't need funding to start. You need to start.
author: based

text: Compounding works for businesses the same way it works for interest. Small, consistent improvements beat moonshot pivots every time.
author: based

text: The hardest part of building a business isn't the technology. It's the selling. Most engineers would rather write code than talk to humans.
author: based

text: Churn is the silent killer. You can celebrate new customers all day, but if the bucket has holes, you're just wasting water.
author: based

text: The best validation is someone giving you money. Everything else — surveys, waitlists, likes — is just flirting.
author: based

text: The aim of marketing is to know and understand the customer so well the product or service fits them and sells itself.
author: Peter Drucker

text: Nobody reads ads. People read what interests them. Sometimes it's an ad.
author: Howard Gossage

text: Content marketing is just storytelling for people who are afraid to ask for the sale.
author: based

text: SEO is the art of making your website slightly less terrible so Google tolerates showing it to people.
author: based

text: The best marketing is word of mouth. The second best is showing up consistently. The worst is buying followers.
author: based

text: Email marketing: because you need a way to annoy people who already told you they're not interested.
author: based

text: A funnel is just a polite way of saying 'we lose most of our customers at each step.'
author: based

text: Social media marketing is just shouting into the void and occasionally the void shouts back.
author: based

text: Brand awareness is what you measure when you can't measure anything useful.
author: based

text: The best marketing strategy is having a product people actually want to tell their friends about.
author: based

text: Influencer marketing is just paying someone to pretend they like your product. The audience knows. They just don't care.
author: based

text: The difference between spam and marketing is whether the recipient asked for it.
author: based

text: Your brand isn't what you say it is. It's what Google says it is. And what Reddit says. And what that one angry customer says.
author: based

text: Content is king. Distribution is god. The graveyard is full of kings nobody visited.
author: based

text: The best time to build an email list was before you launched. The second best time is now. The worst time is never.
author: based

text: Every marketing metric is a proxy for the one that actually matters: revenue. If you can't tie it to revenue, it's decoration.
author: based

text: Testimonials are the most underrated marketing asset. A real person saying 'this worked for me' beats any ad copy.
author: based

text: Cold email works when it's actually about them, not about you. Which means 99% of cold emails don't work.
author: based

text: The best landing page converts at 5%. The average is 2%. Your landing page converts at 0.1% but you think it's 'fine.'
author: based

text: Growth hacking is just marketing with a better haircut.
author: based

text: The best marketing advice is free. Ship something great and tell people about it. Everything else is optimization.
author: based

text: SaaS is just software that you rent instead of own. Like an apartment, except the landlord can change the floor plan while you're sleeping.
author: based

text: The best SaaS businesses are boring. They solve one problem really well and charge monthly for it.
author: based

text: Churn is the tax you pay for not making your product indispensable.
author: based

text: MRR is just a fancy way of saying 'people are paying us.' The fancy part matters less than the paying.
author: based

text: Your pricing page is the most important page on your site. It's where strangers decide if you're a real business.
author: based

text: The best SaaS moat isn't technology. It's habit. Make yourself part of their daily workflow and you're unmovable.
author: based

text: Freemium is a strategy for companies that are confident they can convert you. If you're not confident, it's just giving stuff away.
author: based

text: The best time to add analytics is on day one. The second best time is when you realize you've been flying blind for 6 months.
author: based

text: Feature requests are just customers telling you what they think they want. Usage data tells you what they actually want.
author: based

text: If your onboarding needs a tutorial, your onboarding has failed.
author: based

text: The biggest lie in SaaS: 'We'll add that feature.' You won't. You'll ship what you have and hope nobody notices.
author: based

text: The best SaaS landing page explains what you do in one sentence. If it takes a paragraph, rewrite it.
author: based

text: API rate limits aren't a technical constraint. They're a business model disguised as engineering.
author: based

text: The SaaS customer lifecycle: sign up, use it twice, forget password, reset password, never log in again. Your job is to break this cycle.
author: based

text: Multi-tenant architecture is great until one customer's query takes down everyone else's Saturday.
author: based

text: The hardest part of SaaS isn't building the product. It's maintaining it while building the next version of it.
author: based

text: Usage-based pricing is the future of SaaS because it aligns incentives: you only pay when you get value.
author: based

text: Your free trial exists to sell itself. If it doesn't convert within 14 days, it never will.
author: based

text: SaaS metrics that matter: retention, retention, retention. Everything else is a vanity metric you report to investors.
author: based

text: The best time to build a CLI version of your SaaS is when your power users ask for it. They're your best advocates.
author: based

text: Every SaaS goes through the same phases: 'We'll keep it simple' → 'We need an enterprise tier' → 'What is a microservice?'
author: based

text: The best way to reduce support tickets isn't a better help center. It's a product that doesn't confuse people.
author: based

text: SOC 2 compliance is proof that you filled out a lot of paperwork, not proof that you're secure.
author: based

text: The 'cancel flow' is where SaaS companies reveal their true character. Make it easy and people will come back. Make it hard and they'll never come back.
author: based

text: API-first SaaS companies think in integrations. API-last SaaS companies think in features. Guess which one wins.
author: based

text: The best SaaS partnerships are the ones where both companies' customers benefit. Everything else is just cross-promotion.
author: based

text: Indie hackers don't have exit strategies. They have 'make enough money to not get a real job' strategies.
author: based

text: The best indie hacker metric is revenue per hour of work. Everything else is a distraction.
author: based

text: The beauty of being a solo founder is that you can ship in 20 minutes what takes a committee 3 months. The curse is that you have to do everything.
author: based

text: Ship fast, charge early, iterate often. This is the indie hacker's trifecta. Pick all three.
author: based

text: The best thing about building for yourself: no stakeholder meetings. The worst: you are the stakeholder.
author: based

text: Building in public is just showing your work. But it also means everyone sees your mistakes, your pivots, and your 3 AM commits.
author: based

text: The indie hacker's greatest superpower isn't coding. It's the ability to make decisions without asking permission.
author: based

text: Your first $1k MRR feels like conquering the world. Your first $10k MRR feels like a Tuesday. The journey between is where you learn.
author: based

text: The best marketing for an indie project is being useful. Be useful in forums, be useful on Twitter, be useful everywhere.
author: based

text: Side projects are the best way to learn. Main projects are the best way to earn. You need both.
author: based

text: The indie hacker's nightmare isn't failure. It's spending 2 years building something that gets 3 users.
author: based

text: The best indie businesses are ones you can run from a laptop, in a coffee shop, while ignoring everyone around you.
author: based

text: Every indie hacker who says 'I'll never hire' eventually hires someone. The smart ones wait until they absolutely have to.
author: based

text: The best tech stack for an indie project is the one you can ship with today. Not the one you'll learn eventually.
author: based

text: Indie hacking is just freelancing with equity. Both involve wearing 47 hats. The equity part is just hope in disguise.
author: based

text: The best time to launch is when you have something that works. The worst time is when you think it's perfect.
author: based

text: Your first product taught you everything. Your second product teaches you what actually matters.
author: based

text: Saying 'I'm building a SaaS' is cooler than saying 'I'm building a website.' But they're the same thing.
author: based

text: The best indie hackers are the ones who build things they personally use. They're their own best customer.
author: based

text: One-person businesses are the future. Not because they're trendy, but because leverage makes it possible.
author: based

text: The most underrated indie hacker skill is copywriting. If you can write a clear landing page, you can sell anything.
author: based

text: Indie hackers: 'I want to build something that makes $10k MRR.' Reality: you build something that makes $100 MRR and you're thrilled.
author: based

text: The best time to add Stripe is before you launch. You'd be amazed how many people don't launch because they can't figure out payments.
author: based

text: Ship it, then fix it. Nobody sees your bugs until you've told them where to look.
author: based

text: The customer is always right about the problem. They're almost always wrong about the solution.
author: based

text: If you ask users what they want, they'll say a faster horse. Your job is to build the car.
author: based

text: Watch what users do, not what they say. They'll tell you they use your product daily. Their login history tells a different story.
author: based

text: The user who submits a bug report with screenshots and steps to reproduce is worth their weight in gold.
author: based

text: Requirements are like nose hair: everyone has them, nobody wants to see them, and they keep growing.
author: based

text: The problem with requirements is that they're written by people who don't have to implement them.
author: based

text: Every 'easy' feature request is just code someone else doesn't have to write.
author: based

text: Edge cases aren't edge cases when you have enough users. They're Tuesday.
author: based

text: The user who says 'it's just a simple feature' has never written a line of code in their life.
author: based

text: If you build exactly what the customer asked for, they'll complain it doesn't work the way they actually need it to.
author: based

text: The most important user is the one who's about to leave. Build for them first.
author: based

text: User feedback is data. But data without context is just noise. Always ask 'why?'
author: based

text: The best product decisions come from observing users, not surveying them. Surveys measure opinions. Observations measure behavior.
author: based

text: Every product team says they're 'user-focused.' Then they prioritize internal metrics over user needs.
author: based

text: The best onboarding is invisible. The user shouldn't even know they're being onboarded.
author: based

text: Users don't read. They scan, they click, they get frustrated. Design for that reality.
author: based

text: The best error message is one that tells the user what to do next. The worst tells them what the developer did wrong.
author: based

text: If your product has a 'getting started' guide that's 47 pages long, your product is too complicated.
author: based

text: The best feature is the one you don't build. Every feature is a future maintenance burden and a current cognitive load.
author: based

text: Happy path development is what you do when you've never met a user. Users don't take happy paths. They take confused paths.
author: based

text: The best way to understand your users is to be confused by your own product for 5 minutes. Then fix everything that confused you.
author: based

text: The best code you'll ever write is the code you delete.
author: based

text: Every time you copy-paste code, a unit test dies.
author: based

text: Debugging is like being a detective in a crime movie where you're also the suspect.
author: based

text: If your code works, don't touch it. If it doesn't work, maybe also don't touch it.
author: based

text: The best architecture is the one that doesn't need an architecture diagram.
author: based

text: A function that does one thing well is worth a thousand functions that do everything poorly.
author: based

text: Git blame isn't about finding who's responsible. It's about finding who's qualified to explain what this code was supposed to do.
